seoTitle: How to Pin a Report to a Dashboard in Power BI Service
metaDescription: Learn the basics of pinning a Power BI report to a dashboard in the Power BI service so key metrics are visible in one place.
intro: Dashboards give stakeholders a single view of the metrics that matter most, and getting your reports onto one starts with pinning. This tutorial covers the basics of pinning a report to a dashboard in the Power BI service, plus tips for organizing what you pin.

steps.heading: Open your report in Power BI service
steps.body: Navigate to the report you want to feature and open it within the Power BI service, not just Power BI Desktop.

steps.heading: Pin the visual or page
steps.body: Select the visual or entire page you want on your dashboard and use the pin option to add it there.

steps.heading: Organize your dashboard layout
steps.body: Arrange the pinned tiles so the most important metrics are visible first, adjusting size and position as needed.

steps.heading: Use dashboards for ongoing tracking
steps.body: Apply this to scenarios like tracking business performance in real time, where a single dashboard combines visuals from multiple reports.

faq.question: Can I pin visuals from more than one report to the same dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, dashboards can combine pinned tiles from multiple different reports into a single view.

faq.question: Does pinning create a copy of the data?
faq.answer: The pinned tile is a snapshot of the visual that stays connected to the underlying report, so it updates as the data refreshes.

faq.question: Can I unpin a tile later without deleting the report?
faq.answer: Yes, removing a tile from a dashboard doesn't affect the original report it came from.

faq.question: Is pinning available in Power BI Desktop?
faq.answer: No, pinning to a dashboard is a Power BI service feature and requires publishing your report there first.
